verdict.headline: Go bodyboarding: Belgas Early morning — 08:00
verdict.reason: Best combined score from the checked sources: Rare window session, High confidence, with no major blocker for Belgas.

wetsuit.reason: 4/3 remains the safer default for Peniche unless water is warm and wind is light.

best.explanation: Belgas looks like a clean fun-sized north coast window for Rare window, High confidence: 0.6-0.9 m, 141 kJ, 11 s period, workable consistency 59/100, and glassy conditions.  An open beach break where the sandbanks decide everything; this is best for quiet, punchy peaks when Lagide is too small; 10-13s at low tide can still work well on Belgas, so checking exposed beaches makes sense.  Expect: scan from the dunes first because banks and channels shift fast.
best.shortExplanation: Belgas is an open beach break where the sandbanks decide everything · label Rare window, High confidence · 0.6-0.9 m · 141 kJ · 11 s period · 10-13s at low tide can still work well on Belgas · glassy conditions.

ranked.explanation: Lagide looks like a clean fun-sized window for Fun progression, High confidence: 0.6-0.9 m, 87 kJ, 11 s period, workable consistency 60/100, and glassy conditions.  A dependable left-hand reef that turns modest swell into real rides; this is best for clean, playful walls when the bigger beaches are too wild or too small; 10-13s at mid tide suits the reef shelves and gives long workable walls.  Expect: it can get busy, and it is usually more flow than heavy barrel.
ranked.shortExplanation: Lagide is a dependable left-hand reef that turns modest swell into real rides · label Fun progression, High confidence · 0.6-0.9 m · 87 kJ · 11 s period · 10-13s at mid tide suits the reef shelves and gives long workable walls · glassy conditions.

ranked.explanation: Almagreira looks like a clean fun-sized north coast window for Rare window, Medium confidence: 0.6-0.9 m, 79 kJ, 13 s period, workable consistency 59/100, and glassy conditions.  An exposed north-coast beach break with more raw swell than Baleal; this is best for small, clean north-coast days when protected spots look underpowered; 10-13s at low tide can still work on exposed beaches, so checking exposed beaches makes sense.  Expect: banks, rips and wind exposure decide whether it is magic or messy.
ranked.shortExplanation: Almagreira is an exposed north-coast beach break with more raw swell than Baleal · label Rare window, Medium confidence · 0.6-0.9 m · 79 kJ · 13 s period · 10-13s at low tide can still work on exposed beaches · glassy conditions.

ranked.explanation: Meio da Baia looks like a clean fun-sized window for Worth checking, Medium confidence: 0.6-0.9 m, 99 kJ, 11 s period, workable consistency 60/100, and glassy conditions.  An inconsistent Baía spot that can work well when north-northwest swell reaches the bay; this is best for bodyboard sessions when the bay is clean, there is enough period and size, and the forecast shows a defined wave; 10-13s at low tide can still work on exposed beaches.  Expect: it is less reliable than the main bay spots, so weak swell or short period can make it feel soft.
ranked.shortExplanation: Meio da Baia is an inconsistent Baía spot that can work well when north-northwest swell reaches the bay · label Worth checking, Medium confidence · 0.6-0.9 m · 99 kJ · 11 s period · 10-13s at low tide can still work on exposed beaches · glassy conditions.
